NVDA.title: The Zacks Analyst Blog Highlights NVIDIA, Oracle, Home Depot, Catalyst Bancorp and Hour Loop
NVDA.summary: The Zacks Analyst Blog today features research reports on NVIDIA, Oracle, Home Depot, Catalyst Bancorp, and Hour Loop. The blog provides insights into strengths and weaknesses for each company, highlighting factors like NVIDIA's AI demand versus supply-chain risks, Oracle's cloud growth despite competition, and Home Depot's pro-business momentum against cost pressures. It also covers Catalyst Bancorp's community banking model and Hour Loop's e-commerce strength, while noting associated risks.

NVDA.title: The Zacks Analyst Blog Highlights NVIDIA, Oracle, Home Depot, Catalyst Bancorp and Hour Loop
NVDA.summary: Zacks Equity Research highlights top analyst reports for NVIDIA, Oracle, Home Depot, Catalyst Bancorp, and Hour Loop. The reports detail financial performance, growth drivers, and potential risks for each company, offering insights into their market positions and future outlooks. This content aims to provide investors with key information from analyst evaluations.

NVDA.title: Intel Stock Rips Nearly 6% Overnight After Trump Confirms Apple Chip Manufacturing Deal
NVDA.summary: Intel's stock jumped nearly 6% overnight after former President Donald Trump announced that Apple had agreed to use Intel's foundry services for chip manufacturing in the U.S. This development is seen as a major win for the government-backed chipmaker, which has been undergoing a significant turnaround, and follows months of speculation. Trump also claimed credit for helping Intel secure foundry deals with Nvidia and Elon Musk's upcoming TeraFab, attributing Intel's increased valuation to government intervention.

NVDA.title: Intel Stock Rips Nearly 6% Overnight After Trump Confirms Apple Chip Manufacturing Deal
NVDA.summary: Intel's stock surged nearly 6% overnight after former President Trump announced that Apple had agreed to use Intel's foundry services for chip manufacturing. This deal, reportedly facilitated by the U.S. government, marks a significant turnaround for Intel, which also recently secured deals with Nvidia and Elon Musk's TeraFab. The news follows Intel's 18A-P process entering risk production and contributes to the company's strong year-to-date stock performance and improved financial results, particularly in its Data Center and AI segment.

NVDA.title: Rep. Sells Off Shares of Tapestry, Inc. (NYSE:TPR)
NVDA.summary: A Tennessee Republican representative recently sold between $15,001 and $50,000 worth of Tapestry (NYSE:TPR) stock on June 16th, as part of broader sales across several large-cap companies. Tapestry, a luxury retailer, reported strong quarterly earnings, topping estimates, and affirmed its full-year 2026 EPS guidance. The company also declared a quarterly dividend of $0.40 per share, and analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating with an average price target of $165.12.

NVDA.title: Jim Cramer Names Intel His Favorite Stock, Says Chipmaker 'Will Work' Because Of This Reason
NVDA.summary: Jim Cramer has declared Intel (NASDAQ: INTC) his favorite stock, attributing its potential success to the increasing demand for CPUs in artificial intelligence systems and the company's strong foundry business. Cramer believes the market underappreciates the importance of CPUs in AI infrastructure, projecting a significant rise in the CPU-to-GPU ratio, which benefits Intel as CPUs are its core product. Additionally, geopolitical concerns are prompting companies to seek chip manufacturing alternatives to Taiwan Semiconductor, opening an opportunity for Intel's expanding foundry operations.

NVDA.title: Enbridge Stock: 181% Coverage, AI Data Center Catalyst, 5% Yield (NYSE:ENB)
NVDA.summary: Enbridge, a major North American energy infrastructure company, is poised for record EBITDA and distributable cash flow in 2026 due to robust organic growth and strategic acquisitions. Despite its 14.0X EV/EBITDA, its 31-year dividend growth, 181% dividend coverage, and strategic expansions in areas like the Permian Basin make it an attractive investment, especially with increasing energy demand from AI data centers.

MSFT.title: The Bull Case For Moody's (MCO) Could Change Following New AI And Blockchain Integration Strategy
MSFT.summary: Moody's Corporation has released new open, platform-agnostic AI skills and extended its Model Context Protocol to integrate with platforms like Amazon Quick and Solana. This strategic move allows Moody's analytical data to be directly used by AI agents and tokenized bonds, potentially deepening reliance on its data for financial workflows. The article suggests this could enhance Moody's long-term investment narrative by embedded its intelligence into everyday tools such as Microsoft 365 Copilot Cowork, reinforcing its relevance in an increasingly AI-driven financial world.

GOOGL.title: Apple Will Raise Prices Due To Soaring Memory Costs, CEO Tim Cook Says - Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL), Amazon.com
GOOGL.summary: Apple CEO Tim Cook has announced that the company will raise prices on its products due to soaring memory costs, calling the situation a "100-year flood" in the electronics supply chain. The price increases are a direct result of a dramatic reallocation of DRAM and NAND supply towards AI infrastructure, causing memory and storage chip prices to quadruple. Cook did not specify which devices would be affected or when the price changes would take effect.
